basic.title: An AI Study Partner for Students
basic.zh: 给同学用的 AI 学习助手

card.summary: Turning mistakes into smarter review plans.

content.objective: Explore whether AI can help students notice patterns in their mistakes and decide what to review next, without doing the thinking for them. / 探索 AI 能否帮助学生发现错题规律，并主动决定下一步复习什么。
process: Interviewed classmates about how they currently review mistakes. / 访谈同学现有的错题复习方式。
Mapped the smallest useful workflow: capture, classify, explain, plan. / 设计最小工作流。
Built and tested a lightweight conversational prototype. / 制作并测试对话原型。
content.result: The prototype showed that classification alone was not useful enough. The most valuable moment was when the tool asked the student to explain why an answer was wrong before suggesting a plan.
content.learned: A good learning product should create productive friction. If AI removes every difficult step, it can also remove the moment where learning happens. / 好的学习产品需要保留有效的困难。
content.nextStep: Improve the review workflow and test it with more students. / 完善复习流程，并邀请更多同学测试。

projects.body:
## Why I started

Most study tools give more answers. I wanted to see whether AI could help a student reflect before offering a plan.

## Evidence

Six student tests showed that explanation and reflection were more useful than automatic classification alone.

basic.title: Why Does Short-form Content Hold Attention?
basic.zh: 短内容为什么能抓住注意力？

card.summary: A study of visual rhythm, story and attention.

content.objective: Understand how short-form media holds attention and which techniques can be used without manipulating the audience. / 研究短内容如何留住注意力，以及哪些技巧可以负责任地使用。
process: Collected 30 successful videos across three topics. / 收集三个主题下的 30 个样本。
Coded hooks, cuts, information density and open loops. / 标记开头、剪辑与信息密度。
Re-edited one idea using three different rhythms. / 用三种节奏重新剪辑同一内容。
content.result: The clearest pattern was not speed. Strong pieces created a question early, changed visual information when attention dipped, and delivered a satisfying answer without wasting time.
content.learned: Fast is not the same as clear. The best rhythm follows the idea. / 快不等于清楚，节奏应该服务于想法。
content.nextStep: Apply the findings to an original short video and compare audience retention. / 将结论用于原创短视频并比较留存表现。

projects.body:
## Research question

Attention is shaped by hundreds of tiny creative decisions. I studied rhythm, narrative compression and audience behavior instead of simply judging short-form media as good or bad.

## Main finding

The strongest pieces created a clear question early and changed visual information only when the idea needed it.

basic.title: My First ¥100 from an Idea
basic.zh: 第一次从想法赚到 100 元

card.summary: A tiny experiment in finding and delivering value.

content.objective: Earn a small amount by solving a real problem, without relying on luck, reselling or asking friends to support me. / 尝试通过解决真实问题获得第一笔收入。
process: Listed problems I could solve with skills I already had. / 列出现有能力能解决的问题。
Talked to potential users before building anything. / 先访谈，再制作。
Offered one clear service with a fixed scope and price. / 提供范围与价格明确的服务。
content.result: The first offer was too broad. After narrowing the outcome and showing a sample, the value became easier to understand. One real transaction taught me more than ten imagined business plans.
content.learned: People do not buy effort; they buy a result they understand. / 用户购买的不是努力，而是能够理解的结果。
content.nextStep: Repeat the experiment with a clearer offer and document the customer journey. / 用更清晰的方案重复实验并记录客户旅程。

projects.body:
## The experiment

Revenue is evidence that someone valued the result, not proof that the idea was perfect. I focused on one real need and one clearly scoped service.

## What changed

Showing a sample made the value easier to understand than describing how much work I would do.

basic.title: 30 Days of Thinking in English
basic.zh: 连续 30 天用英语思考

card.summary: One original thought in English, every day.

content.objective: Write one original idea directly in English every day and reduce the habit of translating sentences in my head. / 每天直接用英语写一个原创想法，减少在脑中翻译的习惯。
process: Wrote for ten minutes without using a translator. / 每天十分钟，不使用翻译器。
Marked repeated gaps after writing, not during it. / 写完后再记录表达缺口。
Rewrote one entry each week for clarity. / 每周重写一篇。
content.result: The entries did not become perfect, but they became faster and more personal. Repeated sentence patterns revealed exactly what I needed to learn next.
content.learned: Accuracy matters, but hesitation can become its own habit. Create first, correct second. / 准确很重要，但犹豫也会成为习惯。
content.nextStep: Continue with longer spoken reflections and review the phrase bank monthly. / 继续做更长的口头反思，并每月复习表达库。

projects.body:
## The rule

Fluency grows when English becomes a place to think, not a code to translate. I wrote for ten minutes without a translator and corrected the work only after finishing.

## The result

The writing became faster and more personal, while repeated gaps showed me exactly what to study next.
